name: Struthiomimus
tagline: Built like an ostrich and fast as the wind, this toothless dinosaur was one of the speediest creatures of its age.
intro: This little dinosaur is named "ostrich mimic" for obvious reasons—it looks very bird-like. Struthiomimus, just like its namesake, had a small head, long neck, strong legs, and a toothless beak. It was probably able to run very swiftly after the small lizards and other prey it ate.

topics.title: Compare for Yourself
topics.text: This drawing illustrates how similar Struthiomimus looks compared to a modern ostrich. Struthiomimus was about six and a half feet tall, around the same size as an ostrich. The main differences are that Struthiomimus had no feathers, had a long bony tail, and had clawed hands instead of wings. Maybe you can see why some experts think that birds evolved from dinosaurs.

topics.title: Bird-Like Faces
topics.text: Dinosaurs had very different faces. Although these animals lived in different parts of the world at different times, they all lived during the Mesozoic era, the Age of Dinosaurs.

captions.label: Flyer face
captions.text: Although their faces resemble that of Struthiomimus, Eudimorphodon and Archaeopteryx were both winged reptiles. Many believe Archaeopteryx was the earliest known bird.

captions.label: Carnivore cousins
captions.text: Struthiomimus and Troodon had many similarities. Not only do their faces look alike, but they were both small, swift meat-eaters.
